questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The number of pairs $(m, n)$ of integers such that $n^2-3 m n+m-n=0$ is
questions.solution:
$(3 m+1)(3 n-1)=3 n^2-1$, so if $p$ divides $3 n-1$, then it also divides $3 n^2-1$ and hence also $\left(3 n^2-1\right)-n(3 n-1)=n-1$, and hence also $(3 n-1)-3(n-1)=2$.
So $3 n-1=0, \pm 1$ or $\pm 2$.
But n must be an integer, so $0,1,-2$ do not work. -1 gives $\mathrm{n}=0$ and hence $\mathrm{m}=0$, which is a solution. 2 gives $n=1$ and hence $m=0$, which is a solution.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $P(x)=a x^2+b x+c$ leaves a remainder of 4 when divided by $x$, a remainder of 3 when divided by $(x+1)$ and a remainder of 1 when divided by $(x-1)$ then $P(2)$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & P(x)=a x^2+b x+c \\ & P(x)=Q_1(x)+4=Q_2(x+1)+3=Q_3(x-1)+1 \\ & P(0)=c=4\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & P(-1)=a-b+4=3(b-a=1) \\ & P(1)=a+b+4=1(b+a=3) \\ & b=-1, a=-2\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & P(x)=2 x^2-x+4 \\ & P(2)=-8-2+4=-6\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the equations $p x^2+q x+r=0$ and $r x^2+q x+p=0(p \neq r \neq 0)$ have a negative common root, then the value of $(p-q+r)$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& p x^2+q x+r=0 \\
& r x^2+q x+p=0 \\
& \therefore x=-1
\end{aligned}$
$r x^2+q x+p=0 \text { (on subtract) }$
So common root is -1
$\therefore p-q+r=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If the equations $x^2+2 x+3=0$ and $a x^2+b x+c=0, a, b, c \in R$, have a common root, then $a$ : $\mathrm{b}: \mathrm{c}$ is:
questions.solution:
$x^2+2 x+3=0$
$D=2^2-3 \times 4=-v e$, then both roots are common and coefficient ratio are equal
$\therefore \quad a: b: c=1: 2: 3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\alpha+\beta, \beta+\gamma$ and $\gamma+\alpha$ are the roots of the equation $x^3-3 x-1=0$.
questions.solution: $$ \alpha+\beta+\beta+\gamma+\gamma+\alpha=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \alpha+\beta+\gamma=0 $$ $\therefore \quad$ Roots of the given equation are $-\alpha,-\beta,-\gamma$ Equation whose roots are $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ is $x^3-3 x+1=0$ (P) $\alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2=(\Sigma a)^2-2 \Sigma \alpha \beta=0-2(-3)=6$ (Q) $\alpha^3+\beta^3+\gamma^3=3 \alpha \beta \gamma=3(-1)=-3$ (R) $(\alpha+\beta+\gamma)(\beta+\gamma-\alpha)(g+a-b)=(-2 \alpha)(-2 \beta)(-2 \gamma)=-8(-1)=8$ (S) $\quad\left(\alpha^3-3 \alpha+1\right)\left(\beta^3-3 \beta+1\right)\left(\gamma^3-3 \gamma+1\right)=8$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
